metadata.title: Social Security Payment Schedule 2026 | Check Your Payment Date
metadata.description: Use a simple Social Security payment date checker for 2026 and 2027. Estimate Social Security and SSI payment dates without entering SSN, bank information, or full date of birth.

hero.title: Social Security Payment Schedule 2026
hero.description: Check scheduled Social Security and SSI payment dates by benefit type, birthday range, and month. No SSN, no bank information, and no login required.
hero.tip: Independent tool. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Social Security Administration.

image.alt: Social Security Dates payment schedule checker

checker.title: Check your Social Security payment date
checker.description: Select your benefit type, birthday range, and month to estimate your scheduled payment date from public SSA payment rules.

rules.title: How the Social Security payment schedule works
rules.description: Most Social Security payments are sent on the second, third, or fourth Wednesday of each month, depending on birthday range. SSI and pre-May-1997 cases follow separate rules.

items.title: Birthdays 1st-10th
items.description: Usually paid on the second Wednesday of the month.

items.title: Birthdays 11th-20th
items.description: Usually paid on the third Wednesday of the month.

items.title: Birthdays 21st-31st
items.description: Usually paid on the fourth Wednesday of the month.

items.title: SSI and older cases
items.description: SSI is usually paid on the 1st. Pre-May-1997 Social Security is usually paid on the 3rd.

schedule.title: Fast answers for common payment questions
schedule.description: Start with the checker, then use the guides if you need context for SSI, May payments, or a late payment.

items.title: Choose a month
items.description: Pick any supported 2026 or 2027 payment month.

items.title: Choose your situation
items.description: Select Social Security, SSI, both benefits, or the pre-May-1997 rule.

items.title: Read the estimate
items.description: Use the scheduled date as a planning aid, not as a guarantee from SSA or your bank.

trust.title: Built for privacy and clarity
trust.description: Payment dates are a high-trust topic. This tool stays narrow: it estimates dates only and points you to official SSA channels for benefit, identity, or banking changes.

items.title: No sensitive data
items.description: No Social Security number, full date of birth, bank account, routing number, address, phone, or email.

items.title: Public rules only
items.description: Uses SSA's public payment calendar rules and federal holiday adjustments.

items.title: Not a payment portal
items.description: The site does not enroll direct deposit, update benefits, or contact SSA for you.

items.title: Official links included
items.description: For account, payment method, missing payment, or identity questions, use SSA.gov or GoDirect.gov.

faq.title: Social Security payment date FAQ
faq.description: Short answers for people checking scheduled Social Security or SSI dates.

items.question: When will I get my Social Security check?
items.answer: For most people who started receiving Social Security after May 1997, the date depends on birthday range: second Wednesday for birthdays 1-10, third Wednesday for 11-20, and fourth Wednesday for 21-31.

items.question: When is SSI paid?
items.answer: SSI is usually paid on the 1st of the month. If the 1st falls on a weekend or federal holiday, the scheduled date is usually moved to the previous business day.

items.question: What if I receive both Social Security and SSI?
items.answer: SSA generally schedules SSI on the 1st and Social Security on the 3rd for people who receive both, with weekend and federal holiday adjustments.

items.question: Is this an official SSA tool?
items.answer: No. Social Security Dates is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Social Security Administration.

items.question: What should I do if my payment is late?
items.answer: For electronic payments, SSA says to check with your bank or financial institution first. If you still need help, contact Social Security directly.

cta.title: Check your scheduled payment date
cta.description: Use the checker for a quick estimate, then confirm benefit or account questions only through official SSA channels.
